subject: REQ002: Swarm-Aware SORA Assessment Framework
agenda: 1. Define "swarm-aware" operationally: What interdependencies must SORA address? (coordination, communication, emergent risks)
2. Identify swarm-specific failure modes not covered in single-aircraft SORA
3. Map swarm evidence requirements: What test data distinguishes swarm SORA from single-aircraft?
4. Define SAIL and OSO outcomes for swarm: How does swarm complexity change SAIL levels?
5. Agree on defensibility criteria: What makes swarm SORA assessment defensible to regulators?
6. Document agreed definition and SORA methodology for swarm operations
questions_to_resolve: Dorothy-1: What exactly is "swarm-aware" evidence? What distinguishes it from standard SORA?
Dorothy-3: What criteria make a SORA assessment defensible across multiple SORA assessors?
Flo-2: Does swarm SORA follow JARUS v2.5 section on multi-UAS, or is there a separate methodology?
outputs: SAIL assignment criteria for swarm ops

subject: REQ005: Automatic Containment (Geofence) Design Specification
prerequisites: ODD bounds defined (wind, GPS accuracy, altitude range)
Design standards reference (DO-254, DEFSTAN 00-970 Part 9) reviewed
agenda: 1. Define geofence boundary tolerance: Horizontal (+/-X meters) and vertical (+/-Y meters)
2. Specify fail-safe mechanism: What happens if onboard logic fails? (e.g., altitude limiter independent of GPS)
3. Identify design standard: DO-254 DAL B? DEFSTAN 00-970 Part 9? Custom?
4. Map environmental factors affecting containment: GPS drift, multipath, wind predictions
5. Define verification approach: Monte Carlo? Flight test? Simulation?
6. Agree success criteria: e.g., zero boundary exceedances in 100 Monte Carlo runs across ODD extremes
7. Create containment assurance case structure
questions_to_resolve: Dorothy-4: What are the five design subcriteria for automatic containment? Tolerance, fail-safe, standard, environmental, verification.
Paul-2: What is the onboard logic triggering containment action? What maneuver is executed? (hard stop, RTL, soft stop?)
Milarna-1: What data must be logged to verify containment was working during flight?
outputs: Containment verification plan and test cases

subject: REQ010: Meaningful Human Control & Crew Workload Definition
prerequisites: ConOps draft with crew roles defined
agenda: 1. Operationalize "meaningful human control": What must crew know (situational awareness)? What authority must crew retain?
2. Define situational awareness criteria: Update frequency? Display comprehensiveness? Information quality?
3. Define authority preservation: Which decisions require explicit crew authorization? Which can autonomy decide?
4. Define takeover time requirement: Crew must regain control within X seconds for which off-nominal events?
5. Define workload limits: Max concurrent decisions? Max duty cycle per hour? Fatigue thresholds?
6. Map failure modes where control is lost: Communication loss? Autonomy failure? Recovery strategy?
7. Agree on evidence collection approach for human factors assessment
questions_to_resolve: Dorothy-2: What measurable criteria define "meaningful" human control for autonomous swarms?
Alexa-1: What situational awareness update frequency is required for crew to make informed decisions?
Paul-3: What is the takeover time requirement and who determines it? (CAA? Human factors standard?)
outputs: Meaningful human control definition with measurable criteria
Workload assessment criteria and monitoring plan

subject: REQ006: Link Loss Survivability & C2 Link Assurance
agenda: 1. Define time-to-loss threshold: C2/inter-vehicle link can be lost for how long before action triggered?
2. Define survivable action: Return-to-Launch? Hold position? Safe descent? Crew takeover?
3. Clarify C2 vs inter-vehicle link survivability: Are both independently survivable or jointly survivable?
4. Assume crew intervention or autonomous: Can crew intervene to override default action?
5. Define verification approach: Flight test? Simulation? RF jamming tests?
6. Document authorised and monitored C2 criteria: Who monitors? What triggers non-compliance?
7. Define success criterion: e.g., zero loss-of-separation incidents in X flight hours
questions_to_resolve: Flo-1: What is the time-to-loss threshold and what action is triggered? (Undefined in REQ006)
Paul-4: What happens to the swarm when C2 is lost? Does it maintain formation? Return individually?
Milarna-2: What data must be logged to verify link loss survivability was maintained?
outputs: Link loss time-to-loss and action specification

subject: REQ007: Detect-and-Avoid Predictable Resolution & Swarm Coordination
agenda: 1. Define predictable conflict resolution: Is it deterministic? Foreseeable to crew?
2. Define avoidance maneuver selection logic: Preferred order (climb, turn, descend)? Why?
3. Define swarm coordination during DAA: Do aircraft maintain formation? Take independent action?
4. Define crew awareness and override capability: Can crew override DAA decision?
5. Identify non-cooperative encounter scenarios: Manned aircraft, rogue drones, static obstacles
6. Define verification approach: Flight test? Simulation? DO-365/F3442 compliance?
7. Document success criteria: e.g., zero mid-air collisions in X encounter scenarios
questions_to_resolve: Paul-1: What mechanism defines predictable DAA resolution? Maneuver selection rules must be explicit.
Paul-5: For swarms, does DAA coordination logic prevent inter-swarm collisions as well as swarm-vs-intruder?
Milarna-3: What test scenarios cover non-cooperative encounters? (Unkn aircraft, no coordination)
outputs: DAA maneuver selection logic and swarm coordination rules
DAA verification and test plan (DO-365/F3442 aligned)

subject: REQ009: ML Model Assurance (AMLAS/SACE) & Explainability
prerequisites: ML models identified (vision, coordin, RTL decision)
agenda: 1. Identify all ML models in swarm architecture: Vision, coordination, decision-making, RTL
2. For each model: Define training data, validation data, test data, hyperparameters
3. Define forensic explainability approach: LIME? SHAP? Saliency maps? Model cards?
4. Identify models that cannot be fully explained: Fallback approach? Accept with lower SAIL?
5. Define frozen model manifest: Version, training data hash, validation results, known limitations
6. Define model manifest retention: Duration? Security? Access control? Proprietary data redaction?
7. Create AMLAS case structure and SACE evidence mapping
8. Document fallback if explainability cannot be achieved
questions_to_resolve: Dorothy-6: What explainability techniques are acceptable for safety-critical ML? Perfect explainability is infeasible.
Dorothy-7: What is the frozen-model manifest format? What data must be retained and for how long?
Alexa-2: Which ML models are actually safety-critical vs nice-to-have? Can nice-to-have models fail gracefully?
outputs: AMLAS case structure and safety argument
ML explainability approach and fallback strategy

subject: REQ011: Flight Termination & Contingency Response Matrix
prerequisites: Flight termination system preliminary design available
agenda: 1. Define contingency events: GPS loss, link loss, geofence breach, airspace intrusion, crew incapacitation, system failure
2. Create contingency matrix: Off-nominal event -> action (Return-to-Launch, hold, safe descent, terminate)
3. Define independent termination mechanism: Ground switch? RF kill? Crew joystick?
4. Define time-to-effect for termination: Aircraft must stop within X seconds
5. Define ATSU notification: When is ATC informed of termination? Who informs?
6. Define data preservation: What must be logged before termination?
7. Define verification approach: Ground test? Flight test? Failure mode testing?
8. Document state transitions: What happens in each off-nominal state?
questions_to_resolve: Paul-2: What is the onboard logic for each contingency? Different maneuvers for different failure modes.
Paul-6: What is the termination mechanism? Hard stop (drop aircraft)? RTL? Safe descent?
Milarna-4: How is contingency response verified? What if default action makes things worse?
outputs: Contingency verification plan and test cases

subject: REQ003: Worst-Credible Behavior Definition & Safety Case Structure
agenda: 1. Define credible vs incredible failure modes: Single-failure assumption? Common-mode?
2. Establish worst-credible boundary: Pilot error? Environmental extremes? Sensor saturation?
3. Map failure modes to safety objectives: Each failure must be mitigated or accepted
4. For each worst-credible failure: Identify containment mechanism, backup system, or crew intervention
5. Define assurance levels: DO-254 DAL, DO-178C DAL, or custom confidence level?
6. Create safety case argument structure: Claims, evidence, sub-claims
7. Identify evidence gaps and test plans
questions_to_resolve: Dorothy-5: What boundary separates credible from incredible failures? Methodology needed.
Paul-7: What is the worst-credible environment for the swarm? Rain? Wind? Temperature extremes?
Milarna-5: How is worst-credible behavior verified? Flight test? Simulation? Analysis?
outputs: Failure modes vs safety objectives mapping
Safety case argument structure (GSN or equivalent)

subject: REQ016: Phased Trial Build-Up & Reversible Gates Framework
prerequisites: Phase definitions available (Phase 1 VLOS, Phase 2 BVLOS, Phase 3 swarm)
agenda: 1. Define entry gates for each phase: What evidence must be available to proceed?
2. Define exit gates (go/no-go): What off-nominal events trigger pause or reversal?
3. Define reversal triggers: Off-nominal incident rate > X per hour? Single critical failure?
4. Define reversal process: Who decides? How long to investigate before decision?
5. Define state preservation: If reversal occurs, is Phase N data kept or discarded?
6. Define progress tracking: What metrics show evidence accumulation?
7. Create gate approval authorities: CAA? Applicant? Joint?
questions_to_resolve: Paul-8: What events trigger phase reversal? What is the decision process?
Paul-9: Can the swarm resume Phase N operation after reversal, or must it restart from Phase 1?
Milarna-6: What data is preserved across phase reversals for learning and investigation?
outputs: Phase definition and entry/exit gate criteria
Phase risk dashboard and progress tracking metrics

subject: REQ012 & REQ020: Data Recording, MOR Obligations, Post-Trial Archival
agenda: 1. Define complete recorded evidence: Sensor data scope, video, audio, state logs, timestamp precision
2. Define telemetry sampling rates: Full fidelity for all channels? Compression for some?
3. Define MOR triggers: System failures, incidents, property damage, environmental impact
4. Define MOR filing process: Auto-file or manual? Timeline? Content required?
5. Define DPIA compliance: Personal data collection, retention, access control, breach notification
6. Define post-trial archival: Duration of retention? Security? Access for future investigation?
7. Define forensic investigation support: Can investigators extract any data from archives?
questions_to_resolve: Dorothy-8: What constitutes "complete" recorded evidence? Different meaning than application pack completeness.
Milarna-7: How is telemetry completeness verified? Gaps in data may compromise investigation.
Paul-10: What are the MOR triggers and filing procedures? Regulatory compliance critical.
outputs: MOR trigger criteria and auto-filing logic
Post-trial archival plan and forensic investigation support
